A hypothetical language has the following grammar:

=== START GRAMMAR ===
{grammar}
=== END GRAMMAR ===

It also uses the following orthography and word-shape rules:

=== ORTHOGRAPHY START ===
{orthography}
=== ORTHOGRAPHY END ===

It currently has the following partial lexicon:

=== START LEXICON ===
{lexicon}
=== END LEXICON ===

Add {n} new entries to this lexicon. Create new words that:
1. Follow the orthography rules
2. Are morphologically consistent with the grammar
3. Cover basic vocabulary areas (body parts, family, nature, actions, etc.)
4. Use appropriate derivational processes if specified in the grammar

NEVER invent lexicon similar to English or common languages. Do not repeat existing entries!

Output ONLY the new entries in the same CSV format:
ipa,pos,translation,grammar,derivation,notes

Follow these strict rules:
- EXACTLY six comma-separated fields
- NO header
- NO explanation
- NO commas inside fields
